coat_color_notes: Grey to dark grey with white markings
description: Hardy draught breed from Tamil Nadu adapted to dry semi-arid conditions. Known for endurance and strength in agricultural work.

coat_color_notes: Grey to white with darker head and neck
description: Elite draught breed from Karnataka known for speed and endurance. Originally bred for the Mysore army for hauling equipment.

coat_color_notes: Jet black with occasional white markings on forehead
description: High-yielding buffalo breed from the Banni grasslands of Kutch. Well adapted to arid conditions with excellent milk fat content.

coat_color_notes: Brown to reddish brown with white patches
description: Agile draught breed from the Bargur hills of Erode district. Known for its speed and hill-terrain adaptability.

coat_color_notes: White with red or black spots
description: Hardy breed from the hilly Dangs region. Tolerant to heavy rainfall and humid conditions. Used for both draught and limited milk production.

coat_color_notes: White body with black or red spots on head and neck
description: Dual-purpose breed from the Deccan plateau. Decent milk yield and good draught capability. Known as "Dongari" or "Dongerpati" cattle.

coat_color_notes: Reddish to spotted red-white with distinctive curved forehead
description: One of the principal dairy breeds of India. Highly heat tolerant with excellent disease resistance. Exported worldwide to tropical countries.

coat_color_notes: Grey to dark grey
description: Premier draught breed of South India known for compact muscular build. Progenitor of several other South Indian breeds.

coat_color_notes: Black
horn_notes: Heavy massive horns curving downward then upward
description: Heaviest Indian buffalo breed from Gir forests of Gujarat. Known for high milk yield with excellent fat percentage.

coat_color_notes: Grey to white with red or brown tinge
description: Powerful draught breed from Tamil Nadu. Known for compact muscular body and endurance in heavy agricultural work.

coat_color_notes: Silver-grey to steel grey with darker forequarters
description: Large dual-purpose breed. Known for heavy build and fast trotting gait. Popular for both milk production and draught work.

coat_color_notes: Reddish brown to black
description: Small-sized cattle breed from the Kasaragod district of Kerala. Well adapted to the tropical coastal climate and resistant to local diseases.

coat_color_notes: Grey to dark grey with lighter underbelly
description: Compact draught breed from the Ken river valley of Bundelkhand. Hardy and well-suited to rocky terrain.

coat_color_notes: White to light grey
description: Medium-sized dual-purpose breed from the Kheri district of UP. Known for reasonable milk yield and good draught capability.

coat_color_notes: Varied: black brown red or white
description: Smallest Indian cattle breed from the Western Ghats of Karnataka. Extremely hardy with strong disease resistance adapted to heavy rainfall and hilly terrain.

coat_color_notes: Black to brownish black
description: Important dairy buffalo from North Gujarat. Cross between Murrah and Surti. Consistent milk producer with good fat content.

coat_color_notes: White to light grey with darker extremities
description: Tall well-built draught breed from the Nagaur district of Rajasthan. Prized for fast trotting ability and endurance in desert conditions.

coat_color_notes: Black
horn_notes: Very long flat curved sickle-shaped horns (can exceed 60cm)
description: Distinctive buffalo breed from the Nagpur region. Known for extremely long horns and adaptation to hot dry climate of Vidarbha.

coat_color_notes: Black with white markings on forehead and legs
description: Premier dairy buffalo breed from the Punjab region. One of the highest milk-yielding buffalo breeds in the world.

coat_color_notes: Red to reddish brown with darker extremities
description: Medium-sized dual-purpose breed from the Nimar valley (Narmada basin). Suitable for both draught and moderate milk production.

coat_color_notes: Grey to dark grey
description: Small agile breed from the Madurai-Sivaganga region. Traditionally used in the Jallikattu bull-taming sport. Known for agility and tenacity.

coat_color_notes: Brown with white patches
description: One of the best dairy breeds of Rajasthan from the Bikaner region. Adapted to arid conditions with efficient feed conversion.

coat_color_notes: Reddish to reddish brown with occasional white patches
description: One of the best dairy breeds of the Indian subcontinent. Highly heat-tolerant and tick-resistant. Exported globally to tropical countries.

coat_color_notes: Black to brownish black
description: Small to medium dairy buffalo from the North Karnataka region. Adapted to medium rainfall zone and maintained by small farmers.

coat_color_notes: White to grey
description: Hardy dual-purpose breed from the Thar desert. Among the best milkers of Indian zebu breeds. Well adapted to extreme heat and scarce water.

coat_color_notes: Reddish brown calves turning grey as adults
description: Compact draught breed from the Cauvery delta region. Known for ability to work in waterlogged paddy fields. Important for rice cultivation.
